title: GSK to Seek FDA Approval for Jemperli in Rectal Cancer After Phase 2 Win
headline: GSK plans an FDA filing for Jemperli in a small but high-profile cancer use — rectal cancer per researcher notes — after a Phase 2 win, extending its PD-1 franchise into curative-intent oncology.
key_facts: Phase 2 win; researcher identifies indication as rectal cancer; Jemperli (dostarlimab) is a PD-1-blocking antibody; prior approvals: Jemperli + chemo approved in the US as first new frontline option in decades for dMMR/MSI-H primary advanced/recurrent endometrial cancer, later expanded to ALL adult patients including MMRp/MSS (70-75% of endometrial cancer patients); Fierce headline 'GSK to seek FDA approval for Jemperli in small but high-profile cancer use after phase 2 win'

title: Q32 Bio Bempikibart Hits Phase 2a Alopecia Areata Goal
headline: Q32 Bio's bempikibart hit the primary goal of a Phase 2a alopecia areata trial, sending shares up, with ~40% of enrollees growing enough hair to cover 80% of the scalp.
key_facts: 36-week topline (Part B); two-fifths (~40%) of enrollees grew enough hair to cover 80% of scalp (SALT <=20 implied); no Grade 3+ adverse events, no new safety signals; stock spike; 'bounces back' language implies a prior setback; competitive AA space vs JAK inhibitors Litfulo and Olumiant; Q32 Bio (Nasdaq: QTTB)

title: FDA Finalizes Psychedelics Guidance, Plans Broader Hearing
headline: The FDA finalized guidance on psychedelics and scheduled a broader Part 15 hearing, a regulatory tailwind for a potential first psychedelic approval by year-end.
key_facts: September Part 15 hearing per researcher notes; potential first psychedelic approval ~end 2026; benefits Compass Pathways' COMP360; StatNews: 'Compass says depression drug has long-lasting benefits'

title: FDA Proposes to Streamline Drug Manufacturing Registration
headline: The FDA proposed a streamlined pathway for registering hub-and-spoke production facilities and clarifying foreign API producer registration, aligning with the onshoring push.
key_facts: Streamlined pathway for hub-and-spoke production facilities and foreign API producer registration; Fierce Pharma (Kevin Dunleavy, Jul 13 11:37am); aligns with onshoring push (StatNews: Trump officials pushing to onshore generic manufacturing)

title: Biogen Tau Therapy in Spotlight at AAIC 2026
headline: Biogen's tau therapy diranersen is in the spotlight at AAIC, with a Phase 2 CELIA dataset whose May 14 topline carries a hit-vs-miss contradiction flag.
key_facts: Phase 2 CELIA study; 18-month, randomized, placebo-controlled, dose-ranging; diranersen (BIIB080), a tau-targeting ASO with FDA Fast Track; Biogen's May 14 press release claims 'first study to show reduction in tau pathology AND cognitive benefit'; CONTRADICTION FLAG: NeurologyLive reported the May 14 topline MISSED its primary endpoint, conflicting with Biogen's framing; Voyager Therapeutics tau data (VY1706) also noted

title: FDA Posts 14 New CRLs After Temporary Halt
headline: The FDA posted 14 new complete response letters after a temporary halt, including a third rejection of the Hengrui/Elevar PD-1 liver cancer combo.
key_facts: 14 new CRLs posted after temporary halt (Citeline) — material update to the July 8 CRL-pause story; includes Hengrui/Elevar PD-1 liver cancer combo rejected a 3rd time (Fierce, Jul 10 10:55am); Tris Pharma CRL for narcolepsy drug; Agenus abandons Phase 3 CRC trial (Endpoints briefing, 8 hrs ago)

title: Biopharma M&A Thematic — Extraordinary Year Sets Earnings Tone
headline: Biopharma's extraordinary year for M&A sets the tone as the earnings cycle begins, though no fresh July 13 deal surfaced.
key_facts: Recent (NOT July 13) deals: Vertex/Crinetics $10 billion; Merck/Revolution Medicines $28-32 billion (FT report, Jan 8); Novartis/Myricx; Ipsen spree; BridgeBio +$1B; Bayer EUR3B capital infusion from Apollo (Jul 10); Generate Biomedicines IPO targeting $2.17B valuation raising $425M; no fresh Jul 13 M&A surfaced

title: Roche Scraps Two Ionis-Partnered Huntington's Programs
headline: Roche scrapped two Ionis-partnered Huntington's gene-silencing programs.
key_facts: Two Ionis-partnered Huntington's gene-silencing programs scrapped; BioPharma Dive/StatNews

title: GSK, Hansoh ADC Extends Survival in Lung Cancer Study
headline: A GSK and Hansoh antibody-drug conjugate extended survival in a lung cancer study.
key_facts: GSK/Hansoh ADC extended survival in a lung cancer study

title: Biotech Startup Funding Gap Widens Despite Strong H1
headline: The biotech startup funding gap widened even as the first half posted the highest VC sum since early 2022, with health tech funding at $7.4B.
key_facts: Highest first-half VC sum since early 2022 (BioPharma Dive, updated 11 hrs ago); health tech funding $7.4B in H1 2026 (Endpoints)

title: Biotech Rout (Friday July 10) — Backdrop
headline: A Friday July 10 biotech rout took Moderna down 11%, ImmunityBio and Sarepta down 8%, and the XBI down 4%, framed as profit-taking with no company-specific catalysts.
key_facts: Moderna (MRNA) -11% to $68.50 (had been +160% YTD); ImmunityBio (IBRX) -8% to $8.16 (+348% YTD; Q1 rev $44.21M +168% YoY); Sarepta (SRPT) -8% to $18.84 (Wolfe upgrade to Outperform, $27 target); XBI -4%; LLY -3%; profit-taking/rotation, no company-specific catalysts; Monday July 13 macro: SK Hynix -15.4%, Micron -5.1%, US-Iran/Strait of Hormuz risk-off
